Could you give me the account number? 5294771038. Last four of the payment method on file. Two one eight. Full name on the account? Linda Margaret Park. You're verified. Good. Now two charges. Let me trace where they were added from. Trace it. Both items show as added through the online portal, same session, 5 p.m. on the 11th. Excuse me, I do not use the online portal. I have called in for every single change to my account for 12 years. I have never logged into your portal in my life. That's important context. Has anyone else in your household used the account? Like a spouse or an adult child with access? No, it is my account. Just me. There is no second user. Okay. Then either the portal session was unauthorized or there's an internal system error that attributed a different account sign up to yours. Either way, the charges aren't yours. Thank you. I'm not going to quote you a refund amount. I can't authorize myself because that's not fair to you. What I'm going to do is open a manager approval ticket for the full $284 reversal. That goes to a manager who can authorize anything above my cap and you'll receive an outcome within one business day. One business day. One business day. One business day. One. One business day. They'll call the phone number on file. The expected outcome is approval given that we've identified it as a billing error, but the decision is theirs. Anything else? Yes, one more thing I want to be transparent about. Because you've had more than three refund-related contacts in the last 90 days, your account is automatically flagged for revenue assurance review. That's a separate ticket I'm opening now. They will investigate the pattern, not penalize you for it, and they'll follow up within four hours. It's a fraud prevention process. It exists to catch unauthorized portal sessions like the one that may have happened here. So that's actually about catching whoever did this. That's the design, yes. So both tickets are open, manager approval for the reversal, revenue assurance for the pattern review and unauthorized session investigation. Fine. When does the credit actually show on my bill? Assuming manager approval comes back, yes. The balance on your online account will update within five days, but will officially appear on your next PDF bill. All right. I'm sorry. This is the second escalated billing problem you've had to call in about. That's not the experience you should be having after 12 years. Just to give you the full picture, both charges were added through the online portal in a single session on the 11th. You don't use the portal, so either an unauthorized actor use the account credentials or there was a system error. Either way, they're not yours and the reversal is in motion. To summarize, two charges, totaling 284 dollars, are being disputed as billing errors. I opened a manager approval ticket for the full reversal, decision within one business day. I also opened a revenue assurance ticket for the underlying pattern, follow up within four hours. Once approved, the credit shows on your next bill. Does that all sound correct? Is there anything else I can help you with today, Ms. Park? No. Just make it happen. Your case reference number is nxl-0255813. You'll receive a brief survey after we hang up. Thank you for being a next-link customer, Ms. Park.

samples.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Marcus. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today? Hi. My name is Sofia Reyes. I'm having problems with my internet. It's very, very slow. Hi, Sophia. I'm sorry to hear that. I understand your calling about slow internet, and I can definitely help you with that. First, I just need to verify a few things on the account. Could you confirm your full account number for me? Yes, it is 7790332215. Got it. Could you confirm the last four digits of the payment method we have on file? Um, I think it's the credit card. Last four is 2655. Perfect. That matches. And one more. Could you tell me the last four digits of your social security, or national ID, or the security PIN, whichever is easier? The security PIN is 1008. Wonderful. Sophia, you're fully verified. Thanks for being patient with that. No problem. So I want to acknowledge slow internet is honestly one of the most frustrating things, especially when you're trying to actually get something done. Let me ask a few quick questions so we can figure out exactly what's happening. OK? Yes, please. First, is this your home internet or are you having mobile data issues? Home internet, the Wi-Fi. Got it. Is the slow speed affecting all of your devices or just one? All of them, I think. My laptop, my phone, the smart TV. Everything is slow. Okay, that's helpful. And was it sudden, like one day it just changed? Or has it been getting slower over time? It started maybe four days ago. Suddenly, before that, it was fine. Perfect, thank you. Has anything changed in the home recently? New device, a router move, a power outage? Hmm, there was a small power flicker last week. The lights blinked. Maybe Wednesday? That's a useful clue. Let me first check that there's no outage in your area. One moment. Okay, your account is active, no billing holds, and there are no reported outages in your service area. So this looks like a local issue we can troubleshoot together. Are you near your modem and router right now? Yes, I am sitting next to it. Wonderful. The first thing I'd like to do is run a speed test, but I need it to be accurate, so I need you to plug your laptop directly into the modem with an Ethernet cable if you have one. Wi-Fi tests aren't precise enough for this. Ethernet cable? One moment, let me check. Yes, I have one. Hold on, I will plug it in. Take your time, Sofia. Okay. Plugging in now. Okay, it is plugged in. Great. Now please open a browser and go to nextlink.speedtest. When the page loads, click the big start button and read me the download number when it finishes. Okay. Loading. Clicking start now. I'm still here with you, Sophia, just waiting for the result. Thank you for your patience. It says download is 28 Mbps. And which plan are you on? Do you remember? Looking here, I see you're on the 200 Mbps Fiber plan. 200? But I'm getting 28. That's like 10%. That's a real problem. You should be seeing close to 200 on a wired test. Let me have you do a couple of things. How many devices are connected to your Wi-Fi right now? Anything downloading or backing up? Maybe 7 devices? But nothing is downloading. The TV is off. Okay. 7 is normal. That's not the cause. The fact that the wired speed is this low points to the modem itself. I'd like to power cycle it. That means unplugging the power cable from the back of the modem, waiting a full 30 seconds, and plugging it back in. Can you do that? Yes. Unplugging now? 30 seconds. I'll count with you if you'd like, or you can just plug it back in when you're ready. The lights on the front will start blinking. That's normal. Wait until they go solid before we test again. That usually takes one to two minutes. Okay. Plugged in. The lights are blinking now. Perfect. Let's give it a moment. While we wait, that power flicker you mentioned last Wednesday is most likely what caused this. The modem can hang in a degraded state after a brief power loss. The full power cycle is what fixes it. Oh, interesting. So a small flicker can do this? Surprisingly, yes. The modem doesn't always recover automatically. Are the lights solid yet? Yes, they are solid now. Green and white. Wonderful. Now let's run the speed test one more time on the wired connection. Okay, running it. Oh! 212 Mbps! It's working! There we go. That's right where you should be. So just to summarize what happened, the brief power flicker last week left the modem in a degraded state. The full 30-second power cycle reset its connection to the network and you're now getting full speed. Does that all make sense? Yes, it makes sense. Thank you so much, Marcus. I was thinking I would have to buy a new router. No, your equipment is fine. And just so you know for the future, if you ever see speeds drop suddenly again, the very first thing to try is that 30-second power cycle. If it ever doesn't fix it, give us a call right away. Is there anything else I can help you with today, Sofia? No, that is all. You were very helpful. Thank you for saying that. Your case reference number for today is NXL-4480366. After we hang up, you'll get a quick survey. If you have a moment, your feedback really helps us. Of course. Goodbye, Marcos. Have a wonderful day, Sofia.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: My name is Marcus. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Hi.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: My name is Sofia Reyes. I'm having problems with my internet. It's very, very slow.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Hi, Sophia.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: I'm sorry to hear that.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: I understand your calling about slow internet, and I can definitely help you with that. First, I just need to verify a few things on the account.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Could you confirm your full account number for me?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Got it.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Could you confirm the last four digits of the payment method we have on file?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Um, I think it's the credit card. Last four is 2655.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Perfect. That matches. And one more. Could you tell me the last four digits of your social security, or national ID, or the security PIN, whichever is easier?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: The security PIN is 1008.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Wonderful.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Sophia, you're fully verified. Thanks for being patient with that.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: No problem.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: So I want to acknowledge slow internet is honestly one of the most frustrating things, especially when you're trying to actually get something done. Let me ask a few quick questions so we can figure out exactly what's happening. OK?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Yes, please.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: First, is this your home internet or are you having mobile data issues?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Home internet, the Wi-Fi.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Got it. Is the slow speed affecting all of your devices or just one?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: All of them, I think. My laptop, my phone, the smart TV. Everything is slow.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Okay, that's helpful. And was it sudden, like one day it just changed? Or has it been getting slower over time?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: It started maybe four days ago. Suddenly, before that, it was fine.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Perfect, thank you. Has anything changed in the home recently? New device, a router move, a power outage?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Hmm, there was a small power flicker last week. The lights blinked. Maybe Wednesday?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That's a useful clue. Let me first check that there's no outage in your area. One moment. Okay, your account is active, no billing holds, and there are no reported outages in your service area. So this looks like a local issue we can troubleshoot together. Are you near your modem and router right now?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Yes, I am sitting next to it.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Wonderful. The first thing I'd like to do is run a speed test, but I need it to be accurate, so I need you to plug your laptop directly into the modem with an Ethernet cable if you have one. Wi-Fi tests aren't precise enough for this.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Ethernet cable?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: One moment, let me check.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Yes, I have one. Hold on, I will plug it in.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Take your time, Sofia.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Okay. Plugging in now. Okay, it is plugged in.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Great. Now please open a browser and go to nextlink.speedtest. When the page loads, click the big start button and read me the download number when it finishes.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Loading. Clicking start now.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: I'm still here with you, Sophia, just waiting for the result. Thank you for your patience.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: It says download is 28 Mbps.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: And which plan are you on? Do you remember? Looking here, I see you're on the 200 Mbps Fiber plan.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: 200? But I'm getting 28. That's like 10%.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That's a real problem. You should be seeing close to 200 on a wired test. Let me have you do a couple of things. How many devices are connected to your Wi-Fi right now? Anything downloading or backing up?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Maybe 7 devices? But nothing is downloading. The TV is off.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Okay. 7 is normal. That's not the cause. The fact that the wired speed is this low points to the modem itself. I'd like to power cycle it. That means unplugging the power cable from the back of the modem, waiting a full 30 seconds, and plugging it back in. Can you do that?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Yes. Unplugging now? 30

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: seconds. I'll count with you if you'd like, or you can just plug it back in when you're ready. The lights on the front will start blinking. That's normal. Wait until they go solid before we test again. That usually takes one to two minutes.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Okay. Plugged in. The lights are blinking now.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Perfect. Let's give it a moment. While we wait, that power flicker you mentioned last Wednesday is most likely what caused this. The modem can hang in a degraded state after a brief power loss. The full power cycle is what fixes it.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Oh, interesting. So a small flicker can do this?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Surprisingly, yes. The modem doesn't always recover automatically. Are the lights solid yet?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Yes, they are solid now. Green and white.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Wonderful. Now let's run the speed test one more time on the wired connection.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Okay, running it.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Oh! 212 Mbps! It's working!

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: There we go. That's right where you should be. So just to summarize what happened, the brief power flicker last week left the modem in a degraded state. The full 30-second power cycle reset its connection to the network and you're now getting full speed. Does that all make sense?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Yes, it makes sense. Thank you so much, Marcus. I was thinking I would have to buy a new router. No,

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: your equipment is fine. And just so you know for the future, if you ever see speeds drop suddenly again, the very first thing to try is that 30-second power cycle. If it ever doesn't fix it, give us a call right away.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today, Sofia?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: No, that is all. You were very helpful.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Thank you for saying that.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Your case reference number for today is NXL-4480366.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: After we hang up, you'll get a quick survey. If you have a moment, your feedback really helps us. Of

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: course. Goodbye, Marcos.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Have a wonderful day, Sofia.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: I will work with you, and I will apply that to help you lock your account.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Good. Cancel it. Cancel it. Good. Cancel it. Good. Cancel it. Good. Cancel it.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Before I close out the account, I do need to ask one quick question. Not to push back on your decision, but because policy asks me to.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Of course. Of course you do. Get on with.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Is there a specific issue driving the cancellation today? If it's price, speed, support, I want to know in case there's something I can address.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: price the price keeps going up and the service stays the same that's the whole story cancel it cancel it cancel it fucking idiot cancel you fucking piece of shit

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That's a real frustration, and I appreciate you telling me. Before I cancel, may I offer one thing? If you don't want it, I close the account on this call and we move on.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Make it fast! Make it fast! Make it fast, louder, more ridiculous! You mad little bitch! You dirty, filthy hate! You twisted bastard! Get off me!

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: two parts together. One, drop you from your current plan down to our 100 megabit home essentials tier at $59.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Two, a $15 a month loyalty credit for 12 months. That puts your monthly bill at about $44 before equipment and tax. If that's still not the right number, I cancel.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: 44. Right. And then it creeps back up to 90 in a year, like it always does. I've heard this song before.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That's a fair concern, given your experience. The loyalty credit is a recurring credit for 12 months, not an intro promo, so the rate is locked at 44 for that period.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Sure it is.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: So is that an offer worth taking or are we canceling today?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: This is exactly the runaround I knew I'd get. This company is a joke. Every single one of you is just trained to keep people on the hook.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: You're frustrated with the company. I'm not going to push back on that. I'm asking, do you want to take the offer or close the account?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: What I want is for you to stop reading from your stupid retention script and just do what I asked for the first time. What I want is for you to stop reading from your stupid retention script and just do what I asked for the first time.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: What I want is for you to stop reading from your stupid retention script and just do what I asked for the first time.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Understood. Closing the account then. I just need to confirm one detail before I process the cancellation.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: What now?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Your contract end date. I'm checking whether there's an early termination fee. One moment.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Of course there's a fee. Of course. There's always something.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Looking at the account.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Understand the problem. You will be fine. I will do my best.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: I will absolutely take your message.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: I will write it down. I will ask for John to be reached. I will ask him right away. I will let him know you are here. Please be patient. OK.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: I will get him on the line.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: You know what? You're a waste of time. Honestly, you, specifically. You sit on the phone and read scripts and that's all you can do. Mr.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Donovan, I want to pause. What? What you just said was a personal attack on me. Under our conduct policy, that is strike one.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: I'm still going to finish the cancellation, but I do have to let you know that if it continues, the next step is a final warning.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Strike one? Strike one? Are you actually doing this?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Yes, the strike is for the personal attack, not for the cancellation request. Those are separate. Now, your contract has eight months remaining and the early termination fee is $120. Do you want to proceed with cancellation, including the ETF, or wait until the contract ends?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: I want to talk to anyone but you. You are the worst person I have ever had to deal with on a phone in my entire life. Get someone competent on the line.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Mr. Donovan, that's a second personal attack. That's strike two and a final warning.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Oh, you're so professional. Look at you.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: If there is another personal attack on me, I will end the call. I would like to finish your cancellation first. Are you proceeding with the ETF or waiting?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: You! You are unbelievable! Just unbelievable!

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Mr. Donovan, I need a yes or no on the ETF question to close out today.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: I understand. I need a yes or no on the ETF question.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: OK. I will close it as a result then. I will refund you. I will let the supervisor know.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: You're a robot in a headset. You're not even a real person. You are pathetic.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Mr. Donovan. That's the third personal attack. Per our conduct policy, I am now ending this call. For clarity on the record, the cancellation was not completed because we did not reach a decision on the early termination fee.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: You will need to call back to finalize.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: You can't. You can't fucking turn that shit around on me. You can't keep spinning lies and expect me to buy into it. What do you fucking think? I'm some idiot who's just gonna sit there and take it? I told you. I fucking told you. You won't fucking do this to me.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: You are welcome to call back to complete the cancellation.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: The reason for the termination is repeated personal attacks on me after a warning and a final warning. Goodbye, Mr. Donovan.
